beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee routine at home
beats.reasoning: Early riser habit; 30 years of bus-driver discipline means 6:15am is natural. Fold shower and espresso into single beat rather than separate morning beats.

beats.activity: Commute to Georgetown depot by car
beats.location_name: King County Metro Georgetown Depot (Georgetown, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Steady rain from 7am onward; leaving just before rain starts means dry commute. Georgetown is ~3 miles south; 30 min budgets for light morning traffic and wet roads.

beats.activity: Bus driver shift: morning and afternoon routes in rain
beats.location_name: King County Metro Georgetown Depot (Georgetown, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Typical full shift for Metro driver; steady rain until 2pm means slower service, more stops, patient driving. Shift length ~7.5 hrs is standard. SR-520 closure eastbound 9am–noon doesn't affect surface-street Metro routes, so normal operations. Yesterday's energy (50/100) suggests today he's focused and steady rather than high-energy.
Rain ends by 2pm; commute home ~3 miles, 40 min budgets for post-rain traffic settling and his leisurely post-shift pace. Back home mid-afternoon.

beats.activity: Quick errand: pick up groceries at QFC for dinner prep
beats.reasoning: Local neighborhood errand, 10-min walk each way; he values cooking decent meals and yesterday's salmon dinner was satisfying. Grey day and rain clearing means quick neighborhood shop rather than driving farther.

beats.activity: Walk home from QFC with groceries
beats.reasoning: Short neighborhood walk; budget 20 min to account for wet sidewalks and bags of groceries.

beats.activity: Prep and cook dinner at home
beats.reasoning: After a full shift in the rain, he settles at home to cook. Yesterday's energy and routine suggest he enjoys this wind-down time; preparing a meal ties to his value of good food and quiet reflection.

beats.activity: Eat dinner on porch, watch evening news
beats.reasoning: Rain cleared by mid-afternoon; evening is drizzly but not heavy. He's gregarious but values close friends over crowds; alone time at home with a meal and news is his style. Porch dinner becomes porch with rain-cover or inside—same location.

beats.activity: Phone call with old friend, then read or light TV
beats.reasoning: Gregarious personality but values close friends; evening call fits his style. Keeps it low-key at home rather than heading out on a grey Tuesday. Energy 50/100 suggests he's content with quiet social time.

beats.activity: Evening wind-down: read, tidy kitchen, prepare for bed
beats.reasoning: Second-to-last beat must be wind-down at home before sleep. Early riser discipline means getting to bed by 10:30pm to maintain 6:15am wake-up. Routine maintenance and quiet time before sleep.
8.25 hours of sleep; ends at 05:45am, just before his natural 6:15am wake. Spans overnight visibly. Early riser needs solid sleep after a full rain-day shift.

diary: Woke up to gray skies and that familiar Seattle drizzle. The commute to Georgetown was wet but manageable—nothing like the mess the 520 closure caused for eastbound traffic this morning. Spent most of my shift navigating the rain-slicked streets, watching passengers huddle under the bus shelter awnings. By afternoon the rain had eased up some. Grabbed groceries at QFC on the way home, and the walk back felt good despite my legs being tired. Made a simple dinner on the porch, caught the news while eating. Called Tom around four—we haven't talked in months, and it was nice hearing his voice again. The evening's been quiet since then, just tidying up and getting ready for bed. There's something settling about rainy days, even when they're a bit of a slog.
thought_process: The rain and bridge closure weighed on me more than I expected. Fifty-four years old, and I still feel that pressure when the roads get complicated—not for myself, but for the passengers counting on me to get them where they need to go. The morning rush felt heavier because of the traffic backup; people were anxious. By afternoon, though, I'd settled into it. The routine of dinner prep and calling Tom felt grounding, like I could decompress. I think the day's dampness—literal and atmospheric—made me want connection, which is why I reached out to an old friend. Nothing dramatic, just a Tuesday that reminded me why I appreciate quiet evenings at home.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
